alertitem.name: CORS Misconfiguration
alertitem.desc:
This CORS misconfiguration could allow an attacker to perform AJAX queries to the vulnerable website from a malicious page loaded by the victim's user agent.

In order to perform authenticated AJAX queries, the server must specify the header "Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true" and the "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" header must be set to null or the malicious page's domain. Even if this misconfiguration doesn't allow authenticated AJAX requests, unauthenticated sensitive content can still be accessed (e.g intranet websites).

A malicious page can belong to a malicious website but also a trusted website with flaws (e.g XSS, support of HTTP without TLS allowing code injection through MITM, etc).
alertitem.solution: If a web resource contains sensitive information, the origin should be properly specified in the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header. Only trusted websites needing this resource should be specified in this header, with the most secured protocol supported.

alertitem.name: CSP: script-src unsafe-eval
alertitem.desc: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks. Including (but not limited to) Cross Site Scripting (XSS), and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.
alertitem.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is properly configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

alertitem.name: CSP: script-src unsafe-inline
alertitem.desc: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks. Including (but not limited to) Cross Site Scripting (XSS), and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.
alertitem.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is properly configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

alertitem.name: CSP: style-src unsafe-inline
alertitem.desc: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks. Including (but not limited to) Cross Site Scripting (XSS), and data injection attacks. These attacks are used for everything from data theft to site defacement or distribution of malware. CSP provides a set of standard HTTP headers that allow website owners to declare approved sources of content that browsers should be allowed to load on that page — covered types are JavaScript, CSS, HTML frames, fonts, images and embeddable objects such as Java applets, ActiveX, audio and video files.
alertitem.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is properly configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

alertitem.name: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy Header Missing or Invalid
alertitem.desc: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header is a response header that prevents a document from loading any cross-origin resources that don't explicitly grant the document permission (using CORP or CORS).
alertitem.solution:
Ensure that the application/web server sets the Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header appropriately, and that it sets the Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header to 'require-corp' for documents.

If possible, ensure that the end user uses a standards-compliant and modern web browser that supports the Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header (https://caniuse.com/mdn-http_headers_cross-origin-embedder-policy).

alertitem.name: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy Header Missing or Invalid
alertitem.desc: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header is a response header that allows a site to control if others included documents share the same browsing context. Sharing the same browsing context with untrusted documents might lead to data leak.
alertitem.solution:
Ensure that the application/web server sets the Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header appropriately, and that it sets the Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header to 'same-origin' for documents.

'same-origin-allow-popups' is considered as less secured and should be avoided.

If possible, ensure that the end user uses a standards-compliant and modern web browser that supports the Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header (https://caniuse.com/mdn-http_headers_cross-origin-opener-policy).

alertitem.name: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy Header Missing or Invalid
alertitem.desc: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header is an opt-in header designed to counter side-channels attacks like Spectre. Resource should be specifically set as shareable amongst different origins.
alertitem.solution:
Ensure that the application/web server sets the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header appropriately, and that it sets the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header to 'same-origin' for all web pages.

'same-site' is considered as less secured and should be avoided.

If resources must be shared, set the header to 'cross-origin'.

If possible, ensure that the end user uses a standards-compliant and modern web browser that supports the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header (https://caniuse.com/mdn-http_headers_cross-origin-resource-policy).

alertitem.name: Modern Web Application
alertitem.desc: The application appears to be a modern web application. If you need to explore it automatically then the Client Spider may well be more effective than the standard one.

instance.evidence:
<script type="module">import { injectIntoGlobalHook } from "/@react-refresh";
injectIntoGlobalHook(window);
window.$RefreshReg$ = () => {};
window.$RefreshSig$ = () => (type) => type;</script>
instance.otherinfo: No links have been found while there are scripts, which is an indication that this is a modern web application.

injectIntoGlobalHook(window);
window.$RefreshReg$ = () => {};
window.$RefreshSig$ = () => (type) => type;</script>
instance.otherinfo: No links have been found while there are scripts, which is an indication that this is a modern web application.

injectIntoGlobalHook(window);

window.$RefreshSig$ = () => (type) => type;</script>
instance.otherinfo: No links have been found while there are scripts, which is an indication that this is a modern web application.

instance.evidence:
<script type="module">import { injectIntoGlobalHook } from "/@react-refresh";
injectIntoGlobalHook(window);
window.$RefreshReg$ = () => {};
window.$RefreshSig$ = () => (type) => type;</script>
instance.otherinfo: No links have been found while there are scripts, which is an indication that this is a modern web application.

alertitem.solution: This is an informational alert and so no changes are required.
alertitem.otherinfo: No links have been found while there are scripts, which is an indication that this is a modern web application.

alertitem.name: Storable but Non-Cacheable Content
alertitem.desc: The response contents are storable by caching components such as proxy servers, but will not be retrieved directly from the cache, without validating the request upstream, in response to similar requests from other users.

insight.description: ZAP errors logged - see the zap.log file for details
ZAP warnings logged - see the zap.log file for details
Percentage of responses with status code 2xx
Percentage of responses with status code 4xx
Percentage of endpoints with content type image/svg+xml
Percentage of endpoints with content type text/html
Percentage of endpoints with content type text/javascript
